description: A glance at the sun and a long view down a road both use 'nila' (see). 'somela' (observe) adds deliberate attention to details or patterns, and 'shelomu' (understand) names comprehension. The compound 'thiku nila' (see as small) is established for contempt.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the back of the upper teeth for voiced dental nasal 'n', carry the sound through the nose, and release into high-front 'i'. Move the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', then open into central 'a'. Stress falls on 'ni'.
usage_notes: The seen object precedes this verb. Without an object, 'nila' can report visual ability or the occurrence of sight. A descriptor before the verb can state how something is seen, as in the registered 'thiku nila' (see as small).

semantic_domains.spatial: visual location and relation among things in view
